case.title: NEW YORKER MAGAZINE, INC. v. GEROSA

opinion.h1: NEW YORKER MAGAZINE, INC. v. GEROSA, 356 U.S. 339 (1958)
opinion.h4: NEW YORKER MAGAZINE, INC., v. GEROSA, COMPTROLLER OF THE CITY OF NEW YORK, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 816. Decided April 28, 1958.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Wilbur H. Friedman, Joseph M. Proskauer and Marvin E. Frankel for appellant.
Peter Campbell Brown, Stanley Buchsbaum and Morris L. Heath for appellees.
A brief of amici curiae supporting appellant was filed by Alfred H. Wasserstrom for the Hearst Corporation, and George G. Tyler for Time, Inc.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question. Western Live Stock v. Bureau of Revenue, 303 U.S. 250.
356 U.S. 339 VAN NEWKIRK v. McNEILL, SUPERINTENDENT, MATTEAWAN STATE HOSPITAL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE SECOND CIRCUIT. No. 497, Misc. Decided April 28, 1958.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
